<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Hamiltonians and classical mechanics</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}

      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

← Notebook
1 Phase space 2 Hamilton's equations 3 Canonical transformations 4 Noether 5 Integrable / KAM 6 Worked examples

Hamiltonians and classical mechanics

Phase space is a symplectic manifold and physics is its geometry — Hamilton's equations, Noether's theorem, action-angle coordinates, and KAM, viewed through the cotangent bundle.

blocks.html:
1. Phase space and the cotangent bundle

Newtonian mechanics is second-order in position: $m\ddot q = F(q)$. Re-expressed as a first-order system, it lives on a doubled space — positions paired with momenta. The natural geometric home is the cotangent bundle $T^*Q$ of configuration space $Q$.

$T^*Q$ comes equipped, with no extra choices, with a 1-form: the tautological (or Liouville) form $\theta$. In any chart $(q^i, p_i)$ on $T^*Q$,

$$\theta = \sum_{i=1}^n p_i\,dq^i.$$

Its negative exterior derivative is the canonical symplectic form

$$\omega = -d\theta = \sum_{i=1}^n dq^i\wedge dp_i.$$

Each fibre $T^*_q Q$ is an $n$-dimensional vector space; together $\dim T^*Q = 2n$. The form $\omega$ is closed (since $\omega = d(-\theta)$) and non-degenerate (the matrix $\begin{pmatrix}0 & I\\ -I & 0\end{pmatrix}$ in canonical coordinates is invertible). Phase space is a symplectic manifold by birthright — and the natural coordinates $(q,p)$ are already Darboux.

Phase-space cell
Drag the cell — Liouville's theorem will preserve its area
Area: 1.000 · centroid: (0, 0) · shape: square

The picture above shows a small square in the $(q,p)$ plane evolving under the harmonic oscillator. The shape sheers and rotates, but the area never changes — this is Liouville's theorem (§2) made visible.

Why $T^*Q$ and not $TQ$? Velocities $\dot q$ live in the tangent bundle; momenta $p_i = \partial L/\partial \dot q^i$ are linear functionals on velocities, hence cotangent vectors. The Legendre transform $TQ\to T^*Q$ sends a Lagrangian $L(q,\dot q)$ to a Hamiltonian $H(q,p)$ — when invertible, the two formulations are equivalent. Symplectic structure lives naturally on $T^*Q$ because $\theta$ is built without choosing a metric.

See also
  • Differential forms and wedge product
  • Symplectic form

Used in
Hamiltonians and classical mechanics · Hamilton's equations
</section>

2. Hamilton's equations and the Hamiltonian flow

A smooth function $H\colon T^*Q\to\mathbb{R}$ — the Hamiltonian, usually total energy — generates a vector field $X_H$ defined by the symplectic equation

$$\iota_{X_H}\omega \;=\; dH.$$

Non-degeneracy of $\omega$ makes $X_H$ unique. In canonical coordinates, working out both sides gives the celebrated

$$\dot q^i \;=\; \frac{\partial H}{\partial p_i},\qquad \dot p_i \;=\; -\frac{\partial H}{\partial q^i}.$$

These are Hamilton's equations: a system of $2n$ first-order ODEs replacing the $n$ second-order Lagrangian equations.

Hamiltonian flow on the plane
Pick $H$ and watch the vector field + a trajectory
Click in the field to launch a trajectory.
Click to drop an initial condition.

Two structural facts fall out of the defining identity, almost for free:

Energy conservation. Along the flow, $\dot H = X_H(H) = dH(X_H) = \omega(X_H, X_H) = 0$ since $\omega$ is alternating. Energy is conserved because $\omega$ is antisymmetric — geometry, not a separate axiom.

Liouville's theorem. By Cartan's magic formula,

$$\mathcal{L}_{X_H}\omega = d(\iota_{X_H}\omega) + \iota_{X_H}\,d\omega = d(dH) + 0 = 0.$$

So $X_H$ preserves $\omega$ and therefore its top exterior power $\omega^n/n!$ — the phase-space volume form. Hamiltonian flows are volume-preserving.

See also
  • Orbits, flows, and vector fields
  • Hamiltonian vector fields and flows

Used in
Hamiltonians and classical mechanics · Canonical transformations and Hamilton–Jacobi
Hamiltonians and classical mechanics · Symmetries and Noether's theorem
</section>

3. Canonical transformations and Hamilton–Jacobi

A change of coordinates $\varphi\colon T^*Q\to T^*Q$ is canonical (or symplectic) when it preserves the symplectic form: $\varphi^*\omega = \omega$. Such transformations move Hamilton's equations to Hamilton's equations, possibly with a different Hamiltonian — but they always look like Hamilton's equations.

The cleanest way to manufacture a canonical transformation is via a generating function. Pick $S(q, Q)$ depending on the old position $q$ and the new position $Q$. Set

$$p_i = \frac{\partial S}{\partial q^i},\qquad P_i = -\frac{\partial S}{\partial Q^i}.$$

The graph $\{(q, p, Q, P) : p = \partial_q S,\ P = -\partial_Q S\}$ is a Lagrangian submanifold of $(T^*Q\times T^*Q,\,\omega\ominus\omega)$, and the resulting map $(q,p)\mapsto(Q,P)$ is canonical for free.

The Hamilton–Jacobi equation asks: can we choose $S(q, t)$ so that the transformed Hamiltonian vanishes? If so, the new coordinates are constants of motion and we've integrated the dynamics. The condition is

$$\frac{\partial S}{\partial t} + H\!\left(q,\frac{\partial S}{\partial q},t\right) = 0.$$

A non-linear first-order PDE for one function $S$ — sometimes solvable by separation of variables, sometimes not.

Canonical transformations preserve area
Linear symplectic maps act on the $(q,p)$ plane
$\det J = 1$ — area preserved.
Hamilton–Jacobi $\to$ Schrödinger. Setting $S = -i\hbar\,\log\psi$ in HJ formally yields the Schrödinger equation in the WKB limit $\hbar\to 0$. Canonical transformations are the classical shadow of unitary transformations, and HJ is the classical shadow of the eikonal limit of wave mechanics.

See also
  • Poisson bracket

Used in
Hamiltonians and classical mechanics · Integrable systems and KAM
</section>

4. Symmetries and Noether's theorem

The Poisson bracket on $C^\infty(T^*Q)$ is

$$\{f, g\} = \omega(X_f, X_g) = \sum_i \frac{\partial f}{\partial q^i}\frac{\partial g}{\partial p_i} - \frac{\partial f}{\partial p_i}\frac{\partial g}{\partial q^i}.$$

It makes $C^\infty(T^*Q)$ into a Lie algebra, and along the Hamiltonian flow,

$$\dot f \;=\; X_H(f) \;=\; \{f, H\}.$$

The slogan is short and exact: $f$ is conserved iff $\{f, H\} = 0$.

Each conserved $f$ generates its own flow $X_f$, and $\{f, H\} = 0$ means that flow is a symmetry of $H$. So Noether's theorem in Hamiltonian dress reads:

Continuous symmetries of $H$ ↔ functions Poisson-commuting with $H$ ↔ conserved quantities.

SymmetryGeneratorConserved quantity
Translation in $q^i$$X_{p_i}$Momentum $p_i$
Rotation about axis $i$$X_{L_i}$Angular momentum $L_i = \epsilon_{ijk} q^j p_k$
Time translation$X_H$Energy $H$
Boost (Galilean / relativistic)$X_{K_i}$Centre-of-mass motion
Conserved quantities under symmetry
Watch a quantity evolve along the flow
Pick a system, then a quantity to track.

See also
  • Poisson bracket

Used in
Hamiltonians and classical mechanics · Integrable systems and KAM
</section>

5. Integrable systems, action-angle coordinates, and KAM

A Hamiltonian system on a $2n$-dimensional symplectic manifold is Liouville integrable if it admits $n$ functionally independent first integrals $f_1 = H, f_2, \dots, f_n$ that pairwise Poisson-commute: $\{f_i, f_j\} = 0$. The maximum possible. Their joint flows define a (locally free) $\mathbb{R}^n$-action.

Liouville–Arnold theorem. If a regular common level set $\{f = c\}$ is compact and connected, it is diffeomorphic to a torus $T^n$, and there exist action-angle coordinates $(I_1,\dots,I_n,\theta_1,\dots,\theta_n)$ on a neighbourhood in which

$$\omega = \sum_i dI_i\wedge d\theta_i,\qquad H = H(I)\ \text{(independent of $\theta$)}.$$

Hamilton's equations become trivially soluble: $\dot I = 0$, $\dot\theta_i = \partial H/\partial I_i \equiv \omega_i(I)$. Each torus carries a quasi-periodic linear flow with frequency vector $\omega(I)$.

A 2-torus orbit and KAM perturbation
Two frequencies; rationality / irrationality decides closure
$\omega_2/\omega_1 = 1.610$ — irrational, dense winding.

The KAM theorem. The generic Hamiltonian is not integrable. But integrable systems are not isolated curiosities: under a small perturbation $H_\varepsilon = H_0(I) + \varepsilon H_1(I, \theta)$, the theorem of Kolmogorov, Arnold, and Moser says that most invariant tori survive — specifically, those whose frequency vectors satisfy a Diophantine condition

$$|k\cdot\omega(I)| \;\geq\; \frac{\gamma}{|k|^\tau}\quad\text{for all $0\ne k\in\mathbb{Z}^n$.}$$

The perturbed tori form a Cantor-like set of positive measure. Resonant tori (with rationally related frequencies) shatter into thin Birkhoff zones with chaotic layers — the seeds of chaos in nearly-integrable systems.

What KAM is famous for. KAM was the first rigorous answer to a long-standing question: do small perturbations of the solar system (an integrable Kepler problem at zeroth order) preserve stability? Answer: most planetary tori survive small perturbation. Modern long-time numerical simulations show the inner solar system is in fact in a weakly chaotic regime — KAM applies locally, not over astronomical timescales.

See also
  • Conservative vs dissipative flows

Used in
Hamiltonians and classical mechanics · Worked examples
</section>

6. Worked examples

The canon of classical mechanics is short, and almost every named system is integrable. Each one teaches a different lesson.

The harmonic oscillator

$H = \tfrac{p^2}{2m} + \tfrac{1}{2}m\omega^2 q^2$. Action-angle coordinates: $I = E/\omega$, $\theta = \omega t + \theta_0$. Phase portrait: concentric ellipses, all traversed at the same period $2\pi/\omega$ — the only system besides Kepler with all bounded orbits closed (Bertrand's theorem).

The Kepler problem

$H = \tfrac{|p|^2}{2m} - \tfrac{k}{|q|}$. Conserved: energy $H$, angular momentum $L = q\times p$, and the Laplace–Runge–Lenz vector

$$A = p\times L - mk\,\hat q.$$

Together $(H, L, A)$ give more independent integrals than the dimension allows generically — Kepler is super-integrable. The hidden symmetry is $SO(4)$ (for bound states), and it forces orbits to close: ellipses, parabolas, or hyperbolae, never precessing rosettes.

Kepler orbits
Vary energy and angular momentum to sweep the conics
Ellipse · eccentricity 0.500 · period 6.28

Geodesic flow

On a Riemannian $(M, g)$, take $H(q, p) = \tfrac{1}{2}g^{ij}(q)\,p_i p_j$. Hamilton's equations on $T^*M$ project to geodesics on $M$. Curvature, conjugate points, the Jacobi equation — the geometry of $M$ is the geometry of this flow on phase space.

Convex billiards

Free flight inside a convex region $\Omega\subset\mathbb{R}^2$ with elastic reflection at $\partial\Omega$. The Birkhoff billiard map on $\partial\Omega\times[0,\pi]$ is a 2D area-preserving map, an excellent KAM testbed: caustics are surviving invariant curves, glancing trajectories live near the singular boundary $\sin\alpha = 0$.

What's left out

The three-body problem is famously not integrable (Poincaré, 1890): the failure of the Birkhoff series to converge near a homoclinic point opens the door to chaos. Most named "complicated" systems — the double pendulum, the Hénon–Heiles potential, gravitational $N$-body — sit on the non-integrable side.
</section>

Connections

Classical mechanics is the source of symplectic geometry, but the import runs both ways: every modern advance in symplectic topology — Gromov's non-squeezing, Floer homology, mirror symmetry — was conceived to detect rigidity invisible to the Hamiltonian flow itself. Quantisation maps $\{f, g\}\rightsquigarrow [\hat f, \hat g]/(i\hbar)$, but no fully natural functor from symplectic manifolds to Hilbert spaces exists; geometric quantisation, deformation quantisation, and the Fukaya category are partial answers.

  • Symplectic manifolds — Hamiltonian flow — the abstract setup we specialised here to $T^*Q$.
  • Symplectic manifolds — Poisson bracket — the algebraic shadow of the symplectic form, classical limit of the commutator.
  • Dynamical systems — Conservative flows — Liouville's theorem from the dynamicist's vantage.
  • Differential forms — wedge product — the multilinear algebra underwriting $\omega$.
  • Riemannian geometry — geodesic flow is the cleanest non-trivial Hamiltonian system after the oscillator.

<script>
/* §1 phase-space cell evolving under harmonic oscillator */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("ps-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const SCALE = 60;
  function pix(p){ return [p[0]*SCALE, -p[1]*SCALE]; }
  // initial unit square (corners in (q,p) units)
  const initial = [[-0.5,-0.5],[0.5,-0.5],[0.5,0.5],[-0.5,0.5]];
  let pts = initial.map(p => p.slice());
  let t = 0, playing = false, raf = null;

  // background: ellipses (level sets of H)
  function drawBg(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:-200,y1:0,x2:200,y2:0,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:0,y1:-150,x2:0,y2:150,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:200,y:-6,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"end"})).textContent = "q";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:6,y:-148,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent = "p";
    // level sets H = (q^2 + p^2)/2 = E
    [0.25, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2].forEach(E => {
      const r = Math.sqrt(2*E)*SCALE;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:0,cy:0,r:r,stroke:"var(--line)",fill:"none","stroke-width":1,"stroke-dasharray":"3,4"}));
    });
  }

  function area(q){
    let a = 0;
    for(let i=0;i<q.length;i++){
      const [x1,y1] = q[i], [x2,y2] = q[(i+1)%q.length];
      a += x1*y2 - x2*y1;
    }
    return Math.abs(a)/2;
  }
  function centroid(q){
    let cx=0, cy=0;
    q.forEach(p => { cx += p[0]; cy += p[1]; });
    return [cx/q.length, cy/q.length];
  }

  function draw(){
    drawBg();
    const poly = pts.map(pix).map(p => p.join(",")).join(" ");
    svg.appendChild(SVG("polygon",{points:poly,fill:"rgba(255,216,102,0.18)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.5}));
    // dot at centroid
    const c = centroid(pts);
    const cp = pix(c);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:cp[0],cy:cp[1],r:3,fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
    const A = area(pts);
    document.getElementById("ps-readout").textContent =
      `Area: ${A.toFixed(3)}  ·  centroid: (${c[0].toFixed(2)}, ${c[1].toFixed(2)})  ·  t = ${t.toFixed(2)}`;
  }

  function step(dt){
    // harmonic flow: dq/dt = p, dp/dt = -q (rotation)
    pts = pts.map(([q,p]) => {
      const c = Math.cos(dt), s = Math.sin(dt);
      return [c*q + s*p, -s*q + c*p];
    });
    t += dt;
  }

  function loop(){
    if(!playing) return;
    step(0.04);
    draw();
    raf = requestAnimationFrame(loop);
  }

  document.getElementById("ps-play").addEventListener("click", () => {
    playing = !playing;
    document.getElementById("ps-play").textContent = playing ? "Pause" : "Evolve under H = (p² + q²)/2";
    if(playing) loop();
    else cancelAnimationFrame(raf);
  });
  document.getElementById("ps-reset").addEventListener("click", () => {
    pts = initial.map(p => p.slice()); t = 0; playing = false;
    document.getElementById("ps-play").textContent = "Evolve under H = (p² + q²)/2";
    cancelAnimationFrame(raf); draw();
  });
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* §2 Hamiltonian flow with field + trajectory */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("hf-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const SCALE = 50;
  const sel = document.getElementById("hf-H");
  let traj = []; // list of (q,p) pixel polylines
  let active = null; // active trajectory state

  const Hs = {
    osc:  { f:(q,p)=>(p*p+q*q)/2, dq:(q,p)=>p,    dp:(q,p)=>-q },
    pend: { f:(q,p)=>p*p/2 - Math.cos(q), dq:(q,p)=>p, dp:(q,p)=>-Math.sin(q) },
    duff: { f:(q,p)=>p*p/2 + q*q*q*q/4 - q*q/2, dq:(q,p)=>p, dp:(q,p)=>q - q*q*q },
    free: { f:(q,p)=>p*p/2, dq:(q,p)=>p, dp:(q,p)=>0 }
  };

  function pix(q,p){ return [q*SCALE, -p*SCALE]; }
  function unpix(x,y){ return [x/SCALE, -y/SCALE]; }

  function drawField(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:-220,y1:0,x2:220,y2:0,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:0,y1:-160,x2:0,y2:160,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    const H = Hs[sel.value];
    // grid of arrows
    const step = 36;
    for(let x=-200; x<=200; x+=step){
      for(let y=-140; y<=140; y+=step){
        const [q,p] = unpix(x,y);
        const vq = H.dq(q,p), vp = H.dp(q,p);
        const mag = Math.hypot(vq,vp) || 1;
        const len = Math.min(14, 6 + 8*Math.tanh(mag/2));
        const ux = vq/mag, uy = -vp/mag;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{
          x1:x,y1:y,x2:x+ux*len,y2:y+uy*len,
          stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1,opacity:0.55
        }));
        svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:x+ux*len,cy:y+uy*len,r:1.5,fill:"var(--mute)",opacity:0.7}));
      }
    }
    // a few level curves
    const Nc = 5;
    for(let i=1;i<=Nc;i++){
      const E = (i*0.5);
      // sample by tracing from (q0, 0) where H(q0,0) = E
      // skip — drawing the discrete vector field is enough visually
    }
    // re-draw existing trajectories
    traj.forEach(t => {
      const d = "M" + t.map(([x,y]) => `${x.toFixed(1)} ${y.toFixed(1)}`).join(" L ");
      svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":1.6}));
      const last = t[t.length-1];
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:last[0],cy:last[1],r:3,fill:"var(--cyan)"}));
    });
  }

  function rk4(q,p,dt){
    const H = Hs[sel.value];
    const k1q = H.dq(q,p),         k1p = H.dp(q,p);
    const k2q = H.dq(q+0.5*dt*k1q, p+0.5*dt*k1p), k2p = H.dp(q+0.5*dt*k1q, p+0.5*dt*k1p);
    const k3q = H.dq(q+0.5*dt*k2q, p+0.5*dt*k2p), k3p = H.dp(q+0.5*dt*k2q, p+0.5*dt*k2p);
    const k4q = H.dq(q+dt*k3q,     p+dt*k3p),     k4p = H.dp(q+dt*k3q, p+dt*k3p);
    return [ q + dt*(k1q+2*k2q+2*k3q+k4q)/6, p + dt*(k1p+2*k2p+2*k3p+k4p)/6 ];
  }

  function step(){
    if(!active) return;
    let {q,p,points} = active;
    for(let i=0;i<60;i++){
      [q,p] = rk4(q,p,0.04);
      const [x,y] = pix(q,p);
      if(Math.abs(x) > 240 || Math.abs(y) > 180) break;
      points.push([x,y]);
    }
    active.q = q; active.p = p;
    const H = Hs[sel.value];
    document.getElementById("hf-readout").textContent =
      `q=${q.toFixed(3)}, p=${p.toFixed(3)}  ·  H=${H.f(q,p).toFixed(3)}  (${points.length} pts)`;
    drawField();
  }

  svg.addEventListener("click", (ev) => {
    const rect = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = (ev.clientX - rect.left) * 440/rect.width - 220;
    const y = (ev.clientY - rect.top)  * 320/rect.height - 160;
    const [q,p] = unpix(x,y);
    active = { q, p, points: [[x,y]] };
    traj.push(active.points);
    drawField();
    document.getElementById("hf-readout").textContent =
      `Initial: q=${q.toFixed(3)}, p=${p.toFixed(3)}  ·  H=${Hs[sel.value].f(q,p).toFixed(3)}`;
  });

  document.getElementById("hf-step").addEventListener("click", step);
  document.getElementById("hf-clear").addEventListener("click", () => {
    traj = []; active = null; drawField();
    document.getElementById("hf-readout").textContent = "Click to drop an initial condition.";
  });
  sel.addEventListener("change", () => { traj = []; active = null; drawField(); });

  drawField();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* §3 canonical transformations on a square */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("ct-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const SCALE = 60;
  const sel = document.getElementById("ct-map");
  const param = document.getElementById("ct-param");

  function pix(q,p){ return [q*SCALE, -p*SCALE]; }

  function jacobian(){
    const t = parseFloat(param.value)/100;
    switch(sel.value){
      case "rot":   { const a = t*Math.PI; return [[Math.cos(a), -Math.sin(a)],[Math.sin(a), Math.cos(a)]]; }
      case "shear": { const a = (t-0.5)*4; return [[1, 0],[a, 1]]; }
      case "scale": { const lambda = 0.4 + 1.2*t; return [[lambda, 0],[0, 1/lambda]]; }
      case "non":   { const lambda = 0.4 + 1.2*t; return [[lambda, 0],[0, 1]]; }
    }
    return [[1,0],[0,1]];
  }

  function det(J){ return J[0][0]*J[1][1] - J[0][1]*J[1][0]; }

  function apply(J, pts){
    return pts.map(([q,p]) => [J[0][0]*q + J[0][1]*p, J[1][0]*q + J[1][1]*p]);
  }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:-200,y1:0,x2:200,y2:0,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:0,y1:-150,x2:0,y2:150,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    const sq = [[-1,-0.6],[1,-0.6],[1,0.6],[-1,0.6]];
    // original
    const polyA = sq.map(p => pix(...p).join(",")).join(" ");
    svg.appendChild(SVG("polygon",{points:polyA,fill:"rgba(123,222,214,0.10)",stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":1.4,"stroke-dasharray":"4,3"}));
    // transformed
    const J = jacobian();
    const sqB = apply(J, sq);
    const polyB = sqB.map(p => pix(...p).join(",")).join(" ");
    svg.appendChild(SVG("polygon",{points:polyB,fill:"rgba(255,216,102,0.18)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.6}));
    // labels
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:-200,y:-130,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--cyan)"})).textContent = "original";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:-130,y:-130,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--yellow)"})).textContent = "image φ(R)";
    const d = det(J);
    const ok = Math.abs(d - 1) < 1e-6;
    document.getElementById("ct-readout").textContent =
      `det J = ${d.toFixed(3)}  ·  ${ok ? "area preserved (canonical)" : "area NOT preserved (not canonical)"}`;
  }

  sel.addEventListener("change", draw);
  param.addEventListener("input", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* §4 conserved quantity tracker */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("nt-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const sysSel = document.getElementById("nt-sys");
  const qSel = document.getElementById("nt-quant");
  const W = 440, H = 180;

  function flow(sys, q, p, dt){
    if(sys === "osc"){
      // 1D: dq=p, dp=-q
      const c = Math.cos(dt), s = Math.sin(dt);
      const q0 = q[0], p0 = p[0];
      return [[c*q0 + s*p0], [-s*q0 + c*p0]];
    }
    if(sys === "cent"){
      // 2D central potential, RK4
      function rhs(q,p){
        const r = Math.hypot(q[0],q[1]) + 1e-6;
        return { dq: p.slice(), dp: [-q[0]/(r*r*r), -q[1]/(r*r*r)] };
      }
      function add(a,b,s){ return a.map((v,i)=>v + s*b[i]); }
      const k1 = rhs(q,p);
      const k2 = rhs(add(q,k1.dq,dt/2), add(p,k1.dp,dt/2));
      const k3 = rhs(add(q,k2.dq,dt/2), add(p,k2.dp,dt/2));
      const k4 = rhs(add(q,k3.dq,dt),   add(p,k3.dp,dt));
      const nq = q.map((v,i)=>v + dt*(k1.dq[i]+2*k2.dq[i]+2*k3.dq[i]+k4.dq[i])/6);
      const np = p.map((v,i)=>v + dt*(k1.dp[i]+2*k2.dp[i]+2*k3.dp[i]+k4.dp[i])/6);
      return [nq, np];
    }
    if(sys === "grav"){
      // 1D: dq=p, dp=-1
      return [[q[0] + dt*p[0]], [p[0] - dt]];
    }
  }

  function quantity(sys, q, p){
    if(sys === "osc")  return { H: (p[0]*p[0]+q[0]*q[0])/2, p: p[0], L: NaN };
    if(sys === "cent") return {
      H: 0.5*(p[0]*p[0]+p[1]*p[1]) - 1/Math.hypot(q[0],q[1]),
      p: Math.hypot(p[0],p[1]),
      L: q[0]*p[1] - q[1]*p[0]
    };
    if(sys === "grav") return { H: p[0]*p[0]/2 + q[0], p: p[0], L: NaN };
  }

  function simulate(){
    const sys = sysSel.value;
    let q, p;
    if(sys === "cent"){ q = [1.2, 0]; p = [0, 0.85]; }
    else if(sys === "grav"){ q = [2]; p = [1.5]; }
    else { q = [1]; p = [0.5]; }
    const T = 12, dt = 0.05, N = T/dt;
    const series = [];
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      series.push(quantity(sys,q,p));
      [q,p] = flow(sys,q,p,dt);
    }
    return series;
  }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const series = simulate();
    const key = qSel.value;
    const ys = series.map(s => s[key]);
    if(ys.some(isNaN)){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:0,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"middle"})).textContent =
        "Angular momentum L only defined for the 2D central potential.";
      document.getElementById("nt-readout").textContent = "Pick a 2D system to track L.";
      return;
    }
    const ymin = Math.min(...ys), ymax = Math.max(...ys);
    const yrange = ymax - ymin || 1;
    const pad = 30;
    function px(i){ return pad + (W - 2*pad) * i/series.length; }
    function py(v){ return -((v - ymin)/yrange - 0.5) * 120; }
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:pad,y1:60,x2:W-pad,y2:60,stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:W-pad,y:75,"font-size":10,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"end"})).textContent = "time →";
    // value line
    const d = "M" + ys.map((v,i)=>`${px(i).toFixed(1)} ${py(v).toFixed(1)}`).join(" L ");
    const isConserved = (yrange / (Math.abs((ymax+ymin)/2)+1e-6)) < 1e-3;
    const color = isConserved ? "var(--green)" : "var(--pink)";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d,fill:"none",stroke:color,"stroke-width":1.6}));
    const labelTxt = isConserved ? "conserved" : "varies";
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:pad+8,y:-65,"font-size":11,fill:color})).textContent =
      `${key}(t)  —  ${labelTxt}  ·  range [${ymin.toFixed(3)}, ${ymax.toFixed(3)}]`;
    document.getElementById("nt-readout").textContent =
      `${key} over $t\\in[0,12]$:  min=${ymin.toFixed(3)}, max=${ymax.toFixed(3)}, span=${yrange.toFixed(4)} (${isConserved ? "constant" : "not constant"})`;
  }

  sysSel.addEventListener("change", draw);
  qSel.addEventListener("change", draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* §5 KAM tori on a 2-torus */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("kam-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const w1S = document.getElementById("kam-w1");
  const w2S = document.getElementById("kam-w2");
  const eS  = document.getElementById("kam-eps");

  function gcd(a,b){ while(b){ [a,b]=[b,a%b]; } return a; }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const w1 = parseFloat(w1S.value)/100;
    const w2 = parseFloat(w2S.value)/100;
    const eps = parseFloat(eS.value)/400;
    // unperturbed torus drawn as donut projection
    const Rmaj = 130, Rmin = 50;
    // background disc
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:0,cy:0,r:Rmaj+Rmin,stroke:"var(--line)",fill:"none","stroke-dasharray":"2,3"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:0,cy:0,r:Rmaj-Rmin,stroke:"var(--line)",fill:"none","stroke-dasharray":"2,3"}));
    // trajectory
    const N = 1800;
    let prev = null;
    const ratio = w2/w1;
    // detect near-rational
    const intRatio = Math.round(ratio*1000);
    const g = gcd(intRatio, 1000);
    const num = intRatio/g, den = 1000/g;
    const isRational = (num < 14 && den < 14);
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const t = i*0.04;
      const theta1 = w1*t;
      const theta2 = w2*t + eps*Math.sin(theta1);
      const x = (Rmaj + Rmin*Math.cos(theta2)) * Math.cos(theta1);
      const y = (Rmaj + Rmin*Math.cos(theta2)) * Math.sin(theta1);
      // tilt to suggest 3D
      const yp = y*0.55 - Rmin*Math.sin(theta2)*0.7;
      if(prev){
        svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:prev[0],y1:prev[1],x2:x,y2:yp,
          stroke: eps>0 ? "var(--pink)" : (isRational ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--cyan)"),
          "stroke-width":0.9, opacity:0.85}));
      }
      prev = [x,yp];
    }
    let msg;
    if(eps > 0){
      const sigma = eps * 400;
      msg = `ω₂/ω₁ = ${ratio.toFixed(3)}, ε = ${(eps*400).toFixed(3)} — perturbed torus`;
      if(!isRational) msg += " (Diophantine: survives KAM)";
      else msg += " (resonant: shatters)";
    } else if(isRational){
      msg = `ω₂/ω₁ = ${num}/${den} — rational, closed orbit (period ${2*Math.PI*den/w1*100|0}/100)`;
    } else {
      msg = `ω₂/ω₁ = ${ratio.toFixed(4)} — irrational, dense winding on the torus`;
    }
    document.getElementById("kam-readout").textContent = msg;
  }

  [w1S, w2S, eS].forEach(el => el.addEventListener("input", draw));
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* §6 Kepler orbit */
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById("kp-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const ES = document.getElementById("kp-E");
  const LS = document.getElementById("kp-L");

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const E = parseFloat(ES.value)/100;   // -0.9 .. 0.4
    const L = parseFloat(LS.value)/100;   // 0.2 .. 1.2
    const k = 1, m = 1;
    // semi-latus rectum p = L^2/(mk)
    const sl = L*L/(m*k);
    // eccentricity e = sqrt(1 + 2EL^2/(mk^2))
    const inside = 1 + 2*E*L*L/(m*k*k);
    const e = inside > 0 ? Math.sqrt(inside) : 0;

    // Sun (focus) at origin
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:0,cy:0,r:5,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:8,y:-10,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"})).textContent = "focus";

    // axes hint
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:-200,y1:0,x2:200,y2:0,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-dasharray":"2,4"}));

    // orbit: r(θ) = sl / (1 + e cos θ)
    const SCALE = 80;
    const points = [];
    let kind = "Ellipse", period = NaN;
    if(E < 0 && e < 1){
      kind = "Ellipse";
      const a = sl/(1 - e*e);
      period = 2*Math.PI*Math.sqrt(a*a*a/k);
      for(let theta = 0; theta < 2*Math.PI; theta += 0.02){
        const r = sl / (1 + e*Math.cos(theta));
        if(r > 4) continue;
        points.push([r*Math.cos(theta)*SCALE, -r*Math.sin(theta)*SCALE]);
      }
    } else if(Math.abs(e-1) < 0.05){
      kind = "Parabola";
      for(let theta = -Math.PI*0.95; theta < Math.PI*0.95; theta += 0.02){
        const r = sl / (1 + Math.cos(theta));
        if(r > 4) continue;
        points.push([r*Math.cos(theta)*SCALE, -r*Math.sin(theta)*SCALE]);
      }
    } else {
      kind = "Hyperbola";
      const lim = Math.acos(-1/e) - 0.02;
      for(let theta = -lim; theta < lim; theta += 0.02){
        const r = sl / (1 + e*Math.cos(theta));
        if(r > 4) continue;
        points.push([r*Math.cos(theta)*SCALE, -r*Math.sin(theta)*SCALE]);
      }
    }

    if(points.length){
      const d = "M" + points.map(p => `${p[0].toFixed(1)} ${p[1].toFixed(1)}`).join(" L ");
      svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":1.8}));
    }

    // perihelion / aphelion markers
    if(kind === "Ellipse"){
      const rp = sl/(1+e), ra = sl/(1-e);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:rp*SCALE,cy:0,r:3,fill:"var(--green)"}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:-ra*SCALE,cy:0,r:3,fill:"var(--pink)"}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:rp*SCALE+6,y:14,"font-size":10,fill:"var(--green)"})).textContent = "perihelion";
      svg.appendChild(SVG("text",{x:-ra*SCALE-6,y:14,"font-size":10,fill:"var(--pink)","text-anchor":"end"})).textContent = "aphelion";
    }

    const periodStr = isFinite(period) ? `period ${period.toFixed(2)}` : "unbounded orbit";
    document.getElementById("kp-readout").textContent =
      `${kind} · E=${E.toFixed(2)}, L=${L.toFixed(2)} · e=${e.toFixed(3)} · ${periodStr}`;
  }
  [ES, LS].forEach(el => el.addEventListener("input", draw));
  draw();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/\u00a0/g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>
